metadata.description: Standards bypass configuration for this branch

bypass.reason: Multi-command module where inbox, sent, contacts work without args by design. Adding no-args gate would break primary mail workflow (drone @ai_mail inbox).
This IS the json_handler implementation — cannot import itself.
handle_command() depth 4 — entry point router, nested subcommand dispatch is inherent to the pattern
handle_command() depth 4 — command routing with multiple subcommands, same dispatch pattern
3 functions: check_inbox_for_dispatch() depth 4 (priority scanning with business logic), run_daemon() depth 4 (main daemon loop), _check_lock() depth 4 (lock validation + PID liveness + cleanup)
2 functions: _check_lock() depth 4 (lock validation with PID checks and age), _is_branch_occupied() depth 5 (process inspection, filters by session type — must check /proc)
collect_interactive_input() depth 4 — multiple input() calls each need independent try/except for EOF and KeyboardInterrupt
load_inbox() depth 4 — auto-migration between old and new inbox formats requires nested schema checks

2 functions: get_all_branches() depth 6 (registry parsing with format detection, email derivation, collision handling), deliver_email_to_branch() depth 4 (delivery orchestration with lock acquisition)
mark_read_and_archive() depth 4 — lock context manager + inbox loading + message search + archive
inbox_lock() depth 6 — cross-platform file locking context manager (POSIX fcntl vs Windows msvcrt), platform branching is inherent
2 functions: get_user_by_email() depth 4, get_all_users() depth 4 — registry lookup with path normalization and validation

notes.usage: Add entries to 'bypass' list to exclude specific violations

example.reason: Circular dependency - logger cannot import CLI

fields.standard: Standard name: cli, imports, naming, etc. (required)
fields.lines: Optional - specific line numbers to bypass
